message.content:
<deliberation>
Plan:
- Map each main node to the provided AI Act excerpts.
- Identify any 1-hop cross-references from <referenced_nodes> that give necessary context.
- Check the policy chunk for statements that either contradict, narrow, broaden, or otherwise differ from the regulatory requirement.
- Treat omissions as Non-Deviation per rule 4.
- Only assert deviations where I can cite both a GDPR/Act quote and a policy quote that demonstrate a direct difference.
- Likely deviations: for the three Article 14 items (REG-033, REG-034, REG-035), the policy explicitly states “Applies to: All AI systems,” which broadens scope beyond “high-risk AI systems.” This is an over-compliance (severity) deviation. For other nodes, the policy chunk largely omits relevant details; therefore, per rule 4, classify as Non-Deviation.

Node-by-node reasoning:

1) REG-035 (Art14(3) – oversight measures): Text focuses on human oversight for high-risk AI systems. The policy applies oversight processes to all AI systems (“Applies to: All AI systems.”), which is broader than “high-risk.” This is a severity (over-compliance) deviation. Cross-references relevant to oversight context: REG-032 (Art13(8)(d) human oversight measures in instructions) and REG-045 (Art26(3) deployer’s freedom while implementing provider’s oversight).

2) REG-001 (Art8(1) – general compliance considering state of the art and risk management system): The policy chunk does not address design/development compliance, state of the art, or the linkage to risk management under Article 9. This is an omission; classify as Non-Deviation. Cross-references: full Article 9 chain REG-003, REG-004, REG-005, REG-006, REG-007, REG-009, REG-011, REG-012.

3) REG-034 (Art14(2) – aim of human oversight): Again specific to high-risk AI systems; policy applies generally to “All AI systems.” Over-compliance (severity) deviation. Cross-refs: REG-032 and REG-045.

4) REG-008 (Art9(6) – testing for appropriate risk measures and compliance): Policy chunk does not discuss testing. Omission => Non-Deviation. Cross-ref: REG-009 (real-world testing per Art60) possibly contextual.

5) REG-030 (Art13(6)(iv)-(v) – explanation-relevant information and performance for specific persons/groups): Policy does not cover instructions for use, explanation facilities, or group-specific performance. Omission => Non-Deviation. Cross-refs to Article 13 details: REG-026, REG-027, REG-028, REG-029, REG-031, REG-032.

6) REG-043 (Art26(1) – deployers to use systems per instructions for use): Policy does not mention provider instructions nor technical/organizational measures to ensure conformance to instructions. Omission => Non-Deviation. Cross-refs: REG-045 (Art26(3)) and REG-048 (Art26(6) logs).

7) REG-091: Not provided in main_nodes; cannot quote regulation. Per rule 4.1, classify Non-Deviation. No references.

8) REG-051 (Art26(9) – use Art13 info to comply with GDPR Art35 DPIA obligations): Policy mentions “Impact Assessment” for Sensitive/Restricted Use categorization; it does not state DPIA under GDPR Art35, nor using Art13 info. Omission => Non-Deviation. Cross-refs to Article 13: REG-026..REG-032.

9) REG-025 (Art13(1) – transparency enabling deployers to interpret outputs): Policy chunk does not address transparency features or design. Omission => Non-Deviation. Cross-refs: REG-026..REG-032.

10) REG-050 (Art26(8) – public authorities registration obligation; if not registered, do not use and inform provider/distributor): Policy does not address registration/database checks. Omission => Non-Deviation. No provided cross-refs to Arts 49 or 71; none to include.

11) REG-033 (Art14(1) – design for effective human oversight): Limited to high-risk AI systems. Policy applies oversight processes to all AI systems. Over-compliance (severity) deviation. Cross-refs: REG-032 and REG-045.

12) REG-010 (Art9(8) – testing timing and metrics/probabilistic thresholds): Policy does not cover testing timing or metrics. Omission => Non-Deviation. Cross-ref: REG-009.

For the three Article 14 nodes, I will cite the “High-risk AI systems …” phrasing from the provided texts and the policy’s explicit “Applies to: All AI systems.” For all other nodes, I will mark Non-Deviation due to omission, per the rule that omissions are not deviations.

</deliberation>

    "reg_id": "REG-035",
    "clause": "Art14(3)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-032", "REG-045"],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Severity deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "The oversight measures shall be commensurate with the risks, level of autonomy and context of use of the high-risk AI system...",
        "policy_quote": "Applies to: All AI systems.",
        "mismatch": "The regulation targets oversight for high-risk AI systems, while the policy scopes oversight processes to all AI systems (broader/stricter scope).",
        "analysis": "Policy extends oversight applicability beyond high-risk AI systems; this is over-compliance in scope, not a contradiction."
      }
    ]
